feed.title: John Nichols Ranara
feed.subtitle: Solution-oriented programmer with 10 years of experience in creating software for desktop platforms, mobile development, and hardware.

entry.title: Getting Started with Jekyll
entry.content:
Welcome to my new blog! This is the first post in my Jekyll-powered site hosted on GitHub Pages.

Why Jekyll?

Jekyll is a simple, blog-aware, static site generator perfect for personal, project, or organization sites. Think of it like a file-based CMS, without all the complexity. Jekyll takes your content, renders Markdown or Liquid templates, and spits out a complete, static website ready to be served by Apache, Nginx or another web server.

Key Features

  • Simple: No more databases, comment moderation, or pesky updates to install—just your content.
  • Static: Markdown, Liquid, HTML & CSS go in. Static sites come out ready for hosting.
  • Blog-aware: Permalinks, categories, pages, posts, and custom layouts are all first-class citizens.

Getting Started

To get started with Jekyll, you’ll need to:

  1. Install Ruby (version 2.5.0 or higher)
  2. Install Bundler: gem install bundler
  3. Clone your repository
  4. Run bundle install to install dependencies
  5. Run bundle exec jekyll serve to start the development server

That’s it! Your site will be available at http://localhost:4000.

Stay tuned for more posts about web development, programming, and technology!
entry.summary: Welcome to my new blog! This is the first post in my Jekyll-powered site hosted on GitHub Pages.
